info.name: Bitcoin – Events (POST /token · GET /events · GET /events/status)
info.description:
E2E tests for the bitcoin SSE events feature.

Routes under test:
  POST /api/v1/bitcoin/events/token   — issue one-time SSE JWT (JWTAuth required)
  GET  /api/v1/bitcoin/events         — open SSE stream (cookie auth, origin-gated)
  GET  /api/v1/bitcoin/events/status  — health snapshot (JWTAuth required)

Folder execution order:
  1. setup                — register, verify email (via Gmail), login → events_access_token + events_session_id
  2. token-unauthenticated — POST /token without JWT → 401
  3. token-happy-path     — POST /token → 204 + Set-Cookie: btc_sse_jti
  4. token-rate-limiting  — IP limiter exhaustion: 5 warmup → ok, 6th → 429
  5. events-unauthenticated — GET /events without cookie → 401
  6. events-origin-checks — GET /events bad/missing origin → 403
  7. events-happy-path    — POST /token then GET /events → 200 text/event-stream
  8. status-unauthenticated — GET /status without JWT → 401
  9. status-happy-path    — GET /status → 200 with zmq_connected, rpc_connected, etc.
  10. status-rate-limiting — IP limiter exhaustion: 20 warmup → ok, 21st → 429

Pre-requisites:
  - BTC_NETWORK=testnet4 in .env (collection variable btc_network must match).
  - TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env so X-Forwarded-For rewrites work.
  - ALLOWED_ORIGINS must include the Origin value used in events-happy-path tests.
  - Run `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each run to reset Redis state.
  - Run `make _e2e-db-clean` between runs to remove the setup user.
  - gmail_access_token must be a valid Gmail OAuth2 token set in the Postman environment.

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by internal unit tests):
  - SID HMAC constant-time comparison correctness    — service_test.go
  - IPv4 /24 subnet binding enforcement              — service_test.go (BTC_SSE_TOKEN_BIND_IP=true)
  - JTI replay after ConsumeOnce                     — store_test.go (requires Redis)
  - Redis unavailable paths (503 responses)          — handler_test.go
  - ZMQ unhealthy → 500 on GET /events              — handler_test.go (mock ZMQSubscriber)
  - Process-cap (BTC_MAX_SSE_PROCESS) exhaustion     — handler_test.go
  - Per-user SSE cap race (AcquireSlot after cap check) — service_test.go
  - Broker fan-out correctness / channel draining    — broker_test.go
  - Ping frame format / injection prevention         — handler_test.go
  - RecordTokenIssuance DB miss metric               — service_test.go

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// Rate-limiting folders override _xff in their own prerequest scripts.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, then logs in to obtain events_access_token and events_session_id. All downstream folders depend on these being set.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in email body.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] OTP stored in events_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] events_code is empty — Gmail fetch did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set and not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
// Decode the JWT payload to extract the session_id (sid) claim.
// The sid is embedded in the SSE token and used for server-side session binding.
console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode JWT to extract session_id:', e.message);

item.name: token-unauthenticated
item.description:
POST /events/token without a valid JWT must be rejected before business logic runs.
The IP rate-limiter (btc:token:ip:) runs first (unique XFF from collection prerequest), then deps.JWTAuth rejects.

item.name: token-happy-path
item.description:
Authenticated POST /events/token must return 204 and set a btc_sse_jti HttpOnly cookie.
The cookie is captured in events_sse_cookie for use in the events-happy-path folder.

exec: // Extract the SSE cookie for downstream GET /events tests.
// Postman does not expose HttpOnly cookies via pm.cookies directly in all
// runners; parse from Set-Cookie header as a fallback.
// Store the raw JWT value for the events-happy-path tests.
console.error('[ERROR] Could not extract btc_sse_jti value from Set-Cookie header.');

item.name: POST /events/token — second call reissues a fresh token (idempotent per-call)

exec: // Each POST /events/token produces a NEW one-time JTI in Redis.
// The first token is now stale (its SID key was overwritten by step 4 of IssueToken).
// This test confirms the endpoint accepts repeated calls and issues a fresh cookie.
pm.test('Set-Cookie: btc_sse_jti is present on second call', () => {
// Overwrite events_sse_cookie with the latest token so events-happy-path uses a fresh one.

item.name: token-rate-limiting
item.description:
Verifies the btc:token:ip: IP rate limiter (burst=5, rate=5/60s ≈ 0.083 tokens/sec).
Expected Retry-After: ceil(1 / (5/60)) = ceil(12) = 12 seconds.

All requests in this folder use a fixed XFF (127.0.0.3) to isolate this bucket from other folders.

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (token slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

item.name: events-unauthenticated
item.description: GET /events without the btc_sse_jti cookie must be rejected with 401 before the SSE stream is opened.

item.name: GET /events — no cookie, valid Origin → 401 missing cookie
GET /events — malformed cookie value → 401 token invalid
events-origin-checks
item.description:
GET /events with a missing or disallowed Origin must be rejected with 403 before any JWT/Redis I/O.
Origin check is step 2 of the guard sequence — runs before cookie parsing to avoid timing leaks.

item.name: events-happy-path
item.description:
Full guard sequence for GET /events:
  1. Issue a fresh token via POST /events/token
  2. Connect GET /events with the cookie and a valid Origin
  3. Verify the response opens as text/event-stream with 200
  4. Attempt to replay the same JTI — must return 401 (one-time gate)

NOTE: Postman cannot hold an SSE stream open indefinitely; the GET /events test
verifies response headers (200, Content-Type: text/event-stream) then allows the
connection to close naturally. The stream body is not parsed for events here
because it is non-deterministic (depends on ZMQ live data).

exec: console.log('[INFO] Fresh SSE cookie stored for GET /events happy-path.');
console.error('[ERROR] Could not extract btc_sse_jti from Set-Cookie.');

item.name: GET /events — valid cookie + allowed Origin → 200 text/event-stream

exec: console.error('[ERROR] events_sse_cookie_fresh is empty — POST /events/token happy-path must run first.');
// Postman sends the request and reads the first chunk of the SSE stream.
// The server returns 200 with Content-Type: text/event-stream immediately
// after the guard sequence passes (before any ZMQ event arrives).
// ZMQ may not be running in CI — if the status endpoint reported
// Skip SSE-specific assertions in that case; the guard sequence itself
// is exercised by the successful token issuance above.
console.log('[INFO] ZMQ not running — SSE stream blocked at step 10 (expected in testnet CI).');
// Validate the SSE ping frame is present somewhere in the received body.
// The handler flushes a ping on the first tick; with PingInterval defaulting
// to 30s this may not arrive before Postman's read timeout. The header checks
// above are the authoritative e2e signal; body check is best-effort.
console.log('[INFO] SSE ping frame detected in response body.');
console.log('[INFO] No ping frame in body yet (30s interval) — header checks are the authoritative signal.');
// The same JTI was consumed by the previous GET /events request.
// ConsumeJTI (step 7 of the guard sequence) must reject it with 401.
pm.test('code signals token invalid or already used', () => {
console.log('[INFO] JTI replay correctly rejected with 401.');

item.name: events-rate-limiting
item.description:
Verifies the btc:events:ip: IP rate limiter on GET /events (burst=5, rate=5/60s).
All requests use a fixed XFF (127.0.0.4) to isolate this bucket.
Requests without a valid cookie will still consume rate-limit tokens — the limiter
runs before cookie validation, so warmup requests return 401/403 but count toward the bucket.

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (events slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

item.name: status-unauthenticated
item.description:
GET /events/status without a valid JWT must be rejected before reaching handler logic.
The IP rate-limiter (btc:status:ip:) runs first, then deps.JWTAuth rejects.

item.name: status-happy-path
item.description:
GET /events/status with a valid JWT must return 200 with the StatusResult JSON shape.
The response reflects live ZMQ/RPC state so boolean values are not asserted — only
structure and types are checked.

exec: // Log live values for operator visibility during CI runs.

item.name: GET /events/status — second call returns fresh values (not cached)

exec: // Confirms the handler calls svc.Status on every request (not cached at HTTP layer).

item.name: status-rate-limiting
item.description:
Verifies the btc:status:ip: IP rate limiter on GET /events/status (burst=20, rate=20/60s).
Expected Retry-After: ceil(1 / (20/60)) = ceil(3) = 3 seconds.

All requests use a fixed XFF (127.0.0.5) to isolate this bucket.
Requests without a JWT still consume rate-limit tokens — the limiter runs before JWTAuth.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter for unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set by collection prerequest; overridden in rate-limiting folders.
Must be listed in ALLOWED_ORIGINS in .env. Used for GET /events Origin header.
Set by setup folder from gmail_test_email.
OTP fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script.
JWT access_token from a successful login; used by token and status folders.
Session ID extracted from the access_token JWT payload (sid claim).
Raw btc_sse_jti JWT from the most recent POST /events/token call.
Fresh btc_sse_jti JWT issued in events-happy-path/POST /events/token.
